response.justification: The candidate brings valuable GIS domain expertise, proven ability to architect high-performance distributed systems, and appropriate senior-level experience. However, the critical gap is minimal recent C++ development—their last substantive work in C++ was over a decade ago (2014-2015), with recent years focused on JavaScript/TypeScript and web technologies. While they demonstrate strong learning ability and polyglot skills, immediate productivity in modern C++ for a critical routing pipeline is questionable. Worth a conversation about ramp-up feasibility and technical depth, but represents meaningful risk.
strengths: Proven GIS/geospatial domain expertise from founding GISCollective and Green Map work, providing foundational knowledge of mapping and geodata systems.

Deep distributed systems and high-performance experience from Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP/sec, async I/O, real-time databases), demonstrating understanding of low-latency optimization and fault tolerance.
Senior-level engineer with demonstrated full-stack adaptability, strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, testing frameworks), and willingness to expand expertise across technology stacks.
concerns: C++ experience is stale (2014-2015 National Instruments, thesis work); recent 4 years dominated by JavaScript/TypeScript and web development rather than systems-level programming.
GIS background focuses on data collection/mapping rather than navigation routing optimization or tile generation pipelines—domain expertise gap in the specific problem space.
Recent tech stack (Node.js, TypeScript, React, AWS CDK) differs significantly from the systems programming, performance optimization, and lower-level thinking required for routing data pipelines.
